title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The heart has four muscular pumping chambers, the right atrium, the left atrium, the right

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: ventricle, and the left ventricle.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The left ventricle pumps blood out through a large artery called the aorta to supply

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the body with the oxygen and nutrients it needs.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: When left-sided heart failure, the left ventricle is not able to pump enough blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The two types of left-handed heart failure are diastolic, where the left ventricle can't

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: relax enough to fill with blood, and more commonly systolic, where the left ventricle

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: can't contract strongly enough to push blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: In severe or end-stage heart failure, the heart is getting weaker and doesn't respond

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: to medication.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: For left-sided end-stage systolic heart failure, a doctor may recommend an implantable left

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: ventricular assist device, or LVAD, to help the heart pump enough blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: An LVAD is a small mechanical pump implanted inside the chest or abdomen that takes over

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the job of the weak left ventricle.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: Blood flows from the left ventricle through an inflow tube to the pump.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The pump pushes the blood through an outflow tube into the aorta.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: From here, the blood travels normally from the aorta to the rest of the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: A cable, called a drive line, connects the LVAD pump inside the chest to a control unit

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: located on the outside of the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The control unit is a small computer that controls the functions of the pump and alerts

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the patient if there are any problems.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: The pump and control unit receive power through a cord connected to a battery pack.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: Finally, the control unit and battery pack are held in a lightweight patient pack.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: An LVAD may be a temporary treatment until a heart transplant can be performed, or if

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: the patient isn't able to have a heart transplant, an LVAD may be used for destination therapy,

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
text_en: which is long-term treatment.
